city_report.transport: Il modo migliore per muoversi è attraverso la rete di autobus interurbani e la fitta rete stradale. I treni collegano direttamente il centro con i distretti principali.

attraction.name: Casemates du Bock
attraction.description: Tunnel system part of the city's ancient fortifications.

attraction.name: Grand Ducal Palace
attraction.description: The official residence of the Grand Duke of Luxembourg.

attraction.name: Place d'Armes
attraction.description: Central square in the heart of the old city.

attraction.name: Adolphe Bridge
attraction.description: Double-decked arch bridge and city symbol.

attraction.name: Grund District
attraction.description: Picturesque valley neighborhood below the city center.

attraction.name: Mudam
attraction.description: Museum of modern art designed by I.M. Pei.

attraction.name: Notre-Dame Cathedral
attraction.description: Jesuit church and the only cathedral in the country.

attraction.name: Philharmonie
attraction.description: Modern concert hall on the Kirchberg plateau.

attraction.name: Chemin de la Corniche
attraction.description: Pedestrian promenade called 'the most beautiful balcony in Europe'.

attraction.name: Fort Thüngen
attraction.description: Historic fortification and home of the Dräi Eechelen Museum.

district.name: Ville-Haute
district.description: Il centro storico e politico della città, sede del Palazzo Granducale.

district.name: Grund
district.description: Il pittoresco quartiere medievale sul fiume Alzette, noto per i suoi bistrot.

district.name: Kirchberg
district.description: Il quartiere europeo, sede delle istituzioni UE e del museo MUDAM.

district.name: Clausen
district.description: Quartiere residenziale lungo l'Alzette, famoso per le sue birrerie.

district.name: Limpertsberg
district.description: Quartiere verde e borghese, con l'università e negozi di qualità.

city_report.description: Elite financial center, Luxembourg offers free public transport and one of the highest safety levels in the world. It is the paradigm of the wealthy capital that invests massively in its residents' well-being.
city_report.wiki_intro: The Moselle Valley is one of five regions of Luxembourg, located along the eastern border with Germany. * — capital of the region. * — An old spa town and the only larger destination not located along the Moselle river. * — Former Roman settlement that over time turned into the town it is today. * — Signing place of the Schengen Agreement. * — City on the merging point of the Sûre (Sauer) into the Moselle. * The Moselle Valley is the most eastern territory of Luxembourg, bordering Germany on the other side of the Moselle river. The region lends itself to the cultivation of wines, which is why the hills will be filled with vineyards. The N10 road runs along the Moselle and Sauer rivers, and connects nearly all of the cities along the eastern Luxembourgian border together. When travelling from Thionville or Metz, the road is marked as the D654, which connects to the A31/E25 at Illange. North of the Moselle Valley the N10 connects to the Mullerthal after following the same road as the N1 between Grevenmacher and Wasserbillig. The N10 passes straight through Echternach, then passes by Vianden, only to end near Clervaux. Other routes through the region are the A1, which connects Luxembourg City with Trier via Grevenmacher, and the A13, which connects Esch-sur-Alzette with Merzig and Saarbrücken via Mondorf-les-Bains and Schengen. While none of the cities have a proper train station themselves, on the German side of the Moselle, a train link exists, connecting Perl (Schengen),...
